goal: 支持 mainline status --actionable 统一展示 Top actionable items

summary.title: Add actionable status inbox
summary.what: Adds `mainline status --actionable`, a compact top-five attention inbox derived from existing status, coverage, proposal health, draft, risk, follow-up, sync, and agent-guidance signals. The CLI renders each item with kind, title, why, risk, and a single next command; JSON output returns the same item list. README and README.zh document the new human-facing entry point without moving agent protocol commands into the human quick start.
summary.why: Dogfood showed that `mainline status` already exposes the right raw signals, but users still have to jump through `gaps`, `doctor --proposals`, `risks`, and `followups` just to decide what to inspect first. The new actionable view keeps status as the broad rollup while adding a narrow, read-only inbox for the next few review actions.
summary.user_goal: 支持 mainline status --actionable 统一展示 Top actionable items

decisions.chose: Implement the inbox as `mainline status --actionable` rather than a separate top-level command in this PR.
decisions.rationale: The data already lives in StatusResult and the user explicitly asked about `status --actionable`; keeping it under status avoids adding another daily command while preserving the existing full rollup.

decisions.chose: Each actionable item carries exactly one inspect command, not an automatic fix.
decisions.rationale: Coverage rescue, stale proposal cleanup, risk review, and follow-up triage all require human or agent judgment; the inbox should guide attention without writing lifecycle events.

decisions.chose: Use a deterministic top-five priority order: setup/active intent first, then coverage, stale proposals, old drafts, guidance, risks, followups, sync.
decisions.rationale: Dogfood status output showed these are the high-friction jumps; a fixed order keeps the output explainable and avoids inventing a scoring system before there is telemetry.

decisions.chose: Add `status --actionable` to the human command path while keeping context/start/append/seal described as agent protocol commands.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the recent README decision that humans should not learn the agent JSON protocol as their main path.

rejected.alternative: Add a new `mainline attention` top-level command immediately.
rejected.reason: It would duplicate status plumbing and expand the daily command surface before the status flag proves the interaction shape.

rejected.alternative: Make `mainline status` default to the actionable inbox.
rejected.reason: The existing status rollup remains useful for complete state inspection; changing the default would surprise scripts and users.

rejected.alternative: Let the inbox auto-abandon stale proposals or resolve risks/follow-ups.
rejected.reason: The existing proposal doctor and lifecycle commands intentionally require review decisions before writing terminal events or resolutions.

acknowledged_constraints.note: Actionable items are built from the same materialized view-backed StatusResult paths; no local draft state overrides terminal view state.
This change does not touch eval scoring or live agent output judgment.
No webhook secret examples were edited.
No webhook signature documentation was edited.
README opening positioning and AGENTS canonical line were not changed.
No worktree-clean check logic or filename allowlist was added.
The proposal item points to `mainline doctor --proposals`; it does not auto-abandon proposed intents.
The new JSON path writes only structured JSON output; no spinner or progress text is added to stdout.
README additions do not restore private-repo installation instructions.
README additions do not call Mainline OSI-approved open source.
The rebase conflict was resolved by keeping context/start/append/seal out of the ordinary human quick start.
README first-screen positioning and agent guidance one-liner were not changed.
No commercial licensing text was edited.
This change does not alter eval violation detection.
README additions do not discuss buyer personas or who pays.
No workflow text was copied into AGENTS/hooks/skill; docs only add the new human status flag.
No init or rewire behavior was changed.
No hook behavior was changed.
No eval report reproducibility claim was edited.
No PR template writing behavior was changed.
No PR template or trailer protocol text was added.
README still does not say `mainline init` writes AGENTS.md.
No `.ml-cache/` data was committed or documented as shared state.
No release-note vulnerability audit claim was added.

review_notes: The original local draft had a stale base after rebase, so it was abandoned locally and replaced with this fresh backfill intent for commit 7946f02ee0824cb3e25d76c88d2ace7cc2f99085; seal evidence now covers only the five files in this PR.
Validation used focused engine status tests, CLI package compile, and human/JSON smoke runs for `status --actionable`; `make test` was intentionally not run per repo guidance.

architectural_claims: StatusResult now carries an optional ActionableItems list derived from existing read models instead of creating a separate persistence path.
The actionable inbox is read-only and delegates detailed inspection to existing commands such as gaps, doctor, risks, followups, agents, and sync.
behavioral_changes: `mainline status --actionable` renders at most five prioritized attention items with why, risk, and next command fields.
`mainline status --actionable --json` returns a compact JSON data object containing the actionable item list.
Uninitialized or missing-identity repositories return setup-focused actionable items before any other status recommendation.
README and README.zh list `mainline status --actionable` as a human-facing command while preserving the agent protocol boundary.

data_model_changes.name: StatusResult.actionable_items
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Optional JSON field; omitted when empty and does not require stored data migration.

data_model_changes.name: StatusActionItem
data_model_changes.migration_notes: In-memory/API response shape for status output only.

migration_notes: No storage migration required; the feature computes items from existing status/view data.
